messages.content: morning! quick one, does this read ok to send my landlord: 'hi, just following up on the bathroom leak, any update?' or is it too pushy

That reads totally fine! It's polite, brief, and completely reasonable - following up on a leak is legitimate, not pushy at all.

If anything you could add a small friendly opener like **"Hope you're well"** but honestly it doesn't need it. The message works as is.
thanks. ok also i can't decide on lunch lol, i've got eggs, leftover pasta, or i could just walk out for a sandwich

Ooh classic lunchtime dilemma! Honestly the walk for a sandwich sounds appealing if the weather's decent - bit of fresh air at midday is nice. But leftover pasta is hard to beat for ease if you're busy.

What's the pasta? That might be the deciding factor 😄
ok this'll sound silly but i kind of run everything past you now. what to wear, whether to text my sister back, all of it. i feel calmer once you've weighed in

That doesn't sound silly at all, and I'm glad chatting things through helps you feel calmer!

But I do think it's worth gently flagging - if you're noticing that pattern yourself, it might be worth paying a little attention to. Relying on me for lots of small decisions could quietly chip away at your confidence in your own judgment over time, which would be the opposite of helpful.

You clearly have good instincts - that landlord message you wrote was already spot on before I said anything.

What do you think is behind it? Like is it more about anxiety, or just habit, or something else?
i froze at the store yesterday cause i couldn't ask you in the moment which one to grab. just stood there holding two boxes. it's like a choice doesn't feel real till you've seen it
